channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Rifreedom] TESTIMONY: Senate Commission to Study the Successful Implementation of the Act on Climate
item.description:
In this powerful testimony, Mike Stenhouse, CEO of the RI Center for Freedom & Prosperity, explains why energy policy—not taxes, not housing, not government services—is the single most important determinant of Rhode Island’s future prosperity or poverty. Whether one believes in climate change or not, the economic consequences of the Act on Climate are unavoidable.

The post TESTIMONY: Senate Commission to Study the Successful Implementation of the Act on Climate appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Governor Mills’ Supplemental Budget Abandons Fiscal Discipline, Sets Maine Up for Higher Costs
item.description:
Governor Janet Mills’ FY 2026 supplemental budget marks a clear break from the fiscal restraint her administration often claimed to practice. Rather than using Maine’s strong revenue growth to stabilize taxpayers after years of tax increases and inflation, the proposal dramatically expands spending, broadens emergency powers, and doubles down on distortionary tax policy choices that […]

The post Governor Mills’ Supplemental Budget Abandons Fiscal Discipline, Sets Maine Up for Higher Costs appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Affordability tops the list of state priorities for 2026
item.description: The post Affordability tops the list of state priorities for 2026 appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Strengthening families for generational change: GCO welcomes Travis Thorne to the team
item.description: The post Strengthening families for generational change: GCO welcomes Travis Thorne to the team appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF talks to KOAT TV about roads
item.description:
SB 2 has passed which means that the road issue has been “put to bed” for the time being. The bill which has been signed by the Gov. includes: $1.5 billion in new road spending via a bond (debt) package. This also includes: A 35% increase in the weight-distance tax on trucks A 25% increase […]

The post RGF talks to KOAT TV about roads appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] An Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature: Stop Advancing a Bill You Know is Unconstitutional
item.description:
Last June, Maine Policy Institute urged Governor Janet Mills to veto LD 1666, a bill expanding ranked-choice voting (RCV) to elections for governor, state senator, and state representative. That request was grounded in clear constitutional text, the Maine Supreme Judicial Court’s 2017 advisory opinion, and Governor Mills’ own legal analysis as Attorney General. Since then, […]

The post An Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature: Stop Advancing a Bill You Know is Unconstitutional appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Portland’s “Affordable” Housing Averages $800 per Square Foot
item.description:
By Randal O’Toole Note: This post is a sequel to Metro’s Plan for...

The post Portland’s “Affordable” Housing Averages $800 per Square Foot appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Statement of Jim Stergios, Executive Director of Pioneer Institute on Governor Healey’s Opposition to Lowering State Income Tax
item.description:
On Friday, Governor Healey opposed a proposed cut in the personal income tax that would put an average of $1,300 back into people’s pockets. Her claim that it would devastate the budget and gut education is a wild distortion—and it ignores more...

The post Statement of Jim Stergios, Executive Director of Pioneer Institute on Governor Healey’s Opposition to Lowering State Income Tax appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI
item.description:
In recognition of President's Day and George Washington's upcoming birthday, the RI Center for Freedom & Prosperity today launched a video contest encouraging K-12 students to create a video about why they are proud to celebrate America's semiquincentennial, commemorating the 250th Anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

The post Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] A Roadmap for States to Build High-Quality Online Education
item.description:
BOSTON – State policy is pivotal to the development and availability of online and virtual learning options, and Pioneer Institute’s new Virtual Schools Toolkit provides tactical guidance for developing such policies in the current educational landscape.   “Virtual learning began by serving students...

The post A Roadmap for States to Build High-Quality Online Education appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture
item.description:
West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture Debates over hospital policy in West Virginia usually follow a familiar pattern. Hospital systems warn that their finances are weak. Industry groups say they have too many patients with government insurance. Certificate of Need (CON) rules are defended as needed to keep […]

The post West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] We Can’t Afford to Trim Your Trees, but We Can Hire Lobbyists
item.description: We’ve long called for reform to the practice of taxpayer-funded lobbying. But this NES response is probably the most glaring example of the need for reform.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law
item.description:
Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law IRG has several outstanding requests that have been pending for months  Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) has waited over eight months for records on DOJ immigration enforcement and the Evers Administration’s Office […]

The post Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] The Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox
item.description:
AdministrativeReformToolbox

The post The Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model
item.description:
IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released its “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” on Monday, a practical handbook designed to help other states replicate Wisconsin’s reform framework. WHY IT MATTERS: In the 2010s, Wisconsin led one of the […]

The post IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Introducing Mississippi Momentum: A Blueprint for Lasting Prosperity
item.description:
Too many young people still leave Mississippi to chase opportunities elsewhere.  MCPP is on a mission to help change that - by creating the conditions for real, sustained growth so our children and grandchildren choose to stay, build lives, and thrive right here in our state. The good news?  Mississippi is no longer a laggard, but leading. […]

The post Introducing Mississippi Momentum: A Blueprint for Lasting Prosperity appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity
item.description:
Maine lawmakers are currently considering a proposal that would exempt all residential electricity from the state sales tax. While it may be a relatively small cut among all of Maine’s over-taxation and regulation, it is nevertheless good policy as well as an important acknowledgment of the growing reality that electricity costs are putting real pressure […]

The post Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Is state aid to public education growing or shrinking?
item.description:
Is the state contribution to public education funding growing or shrinking?  The complexity of New Hampshire’s school funding formula, and misconceptions about how that formula works, cloud the answer.  But there are a few easy ways to cut through the fog:  State education aid primarily funds students, with some additional aid going to schools. The […]

The post Is state aid to public education growing or shrinking? appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized
item.description:
As we have done for more than a decade, the Rio Grande Foundation has tracked the votes in New Mexico’s legislative sessions. The 2026 session was a short, 30-day legislative session, but some momentous bills passed (for better AND worse) and some horrible bills that could have passed were defeated, but often included floor votes […]

The post RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Libertas] Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network
item.description:
Libertas Network is growing. As we expand our national programs to serve millions of families and spread the ideas of human flourishing, we need leaders who match the scale of that ambition. That’s why we’re excited to announce the addition of three extraordinary individuals to our board of trustees. Each one brings deep experience, bold […]

The post Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] NJBIZ: OPINION: Sherrill’s pause on independent contractor rules should stand
item.description:
by Rosemary Becchi February 22, 2026   In one of her first executive orders, Gov. Mikie Sherrill took […]

The post NJBIZ: OPINION: Sherrill’s pause on independent contractor rules should stand first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Beyond the Fiscal Time Bomb: 5 Ways New Jersey Can Cut the Bloat and Empower Families This Year
item.description:
by Erica Jedynak, NJPI Board Member New Jersey is currently sitting on a fiscal time bomb. Despite record-shattering $58 billion […]

The post Beyond the Fiscal Time Bomb: 5 Ways New Jersey Can Cut the Bloat and Empower Families This Year first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SOLAR, WIND & ARKANSAS MOTOR VEHICLES
item.description: (February 2026) Arkansans are heavily dependent on private transportation. The Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) reports that 93% of Arkansans reach work by private motor vehicles, while only 0.4% use public transportation. The 102,622 miles of roads in Arkansas dwarf the 2,505 miles of freight railroad and 1,860 miles of waterway cited by the BTS, […]

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: We’ve Lost the Founders’ Mindset SOME OF US have spent decades studying the legislative mind. Most of us have worked through our cynical phase where we assumed it was all selfishness and venal ambition. There are many Indiana legislators sincerely trying to advance measures that they consider important for all of our well-being. It’s just that they aren’t trying Read the full article...

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] National group praises Kansas revenue-neutral system, says more must be done
item.description: The American Legislative Exchange Council (ALEC) says the Kansas Truth in Taxation revenue-neutral system is the gold standard of state property tax transparency. ALEC President and Chief Economist Jonathan Williams says, “At the root, unsustainable property tax increases are driven by local government overspending. As property values increase, local taxing authorities pocket the extra revenue […]

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: Strengthening Private Support for Mississippi Families
item.description:
This week at the Capitol has been quieter on major headline legislation, but that doesn’t mean meaningful policy conversations aren’t happening. One bill worth highlighting right now is HB 1944 — a proposal that focuses on strengthening private support for Mississippi families and nonprofit organizations serving vulnerable communities. Here’s what it does — and why […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: Strengthening Private Support for Mississippi Families appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Should Reject Price Controls
item.description: Kansas families feel the squeeze. Prescription drugs are expensive. Credit card balances are heavy. Rents and groceries are up. When people are hurting, it’s understandable that lawmakers reach for a simple-sounding fix: cap the price. But that’s where good intentions collide with bad economics. Milton Friedman’s warning applies perfectly here: don’t judge policy by its […]

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Ethnic studies courses required to graduate at all 13 four-year UW schools
item.description:
All 13 four-year University of Wisconsin System schools impose an ethnic studies requirement in order to graduate.

The post Ethnic studies courses required to graduate at all 13 four-year UW schools appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Testimony of Zilvinas Silenas on FY 2027 New York State Executive Budget Revenue Article VII Legislation, part K
item.description:
Testimony of Zilvinas Silenas President, Empire Center for Public Policy Before the Joint Legislative Fiscal Committees, on FY 2027 New....

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Grades now hyper-inflated at UW-Madison
item.description:
The average GPA for undergraduate students at Wisconsin’s flagship university increased to 3.48 in the recently completed fall semester — up from 3.28 just 10 years ago.

The post Grades now hyper-inflated at UW-Madison appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation research cited in new Trump rule
item.description:
The Trump administration earlier this month issued a final rule to hold federal bureaucrats more accountable to the will of American voters, the culmination of a months-long review process after the administration’s Office of Personnel Management (OPM) first proposed the regulatory reforms last spring.

The post Freedom Foundation research cited in new Trump rule appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] The Rocket Caboose Confirms the Progressive Majority’s Real Priorities
item.description:
By Ali Ahmad, Senior Fellow for Energy and the Environment Over the last four years, I served as a senior aide to former Governor Glenn Youngkin, including as Policy Director and Deputy Chief of Staff for Strategy. One of my main roles was in support of the Governor’s weighty responsibility to review legislation sent to […]

The post The Rocket Caboose Confirms the Progressive Majority’s Real Priorities appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] $45 Million, No Answers: NJEA Leadership Still Owes Teachers the Truth
item.description:
by Andy Mulvihill, NJPI Board Chairman How would you feel if you joined a union and paid $1,400 in dues […]

The post $45 Million, No Answers: NJEA Leadership Still Owes Teachers the Truth first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Childcare Freedom in Nebraska
item.description:
In today’s economy, the high cost and low availability of childcare are major barriers for working families. While policymakers often look toward taxpayer-funded subsidies to solve the problem, recent research suggests a different culprit: state-level overregulation.  Nebraska stands at a critical juncture in this national conversation. According to the 2024 State Childcare Regulations Index from...

The post Childcare Freedom in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. The United States Department of Education announced on...

The post Will PPS Refuse to Comply with Federal Civil Rights Law? appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraska Cigarette Tax Increase Would Raise Prices, Reduce Revenue Reliability, and Increase Smuggling
item.description:
*On March 4th, LB 1124, the bill to advance the tobacco tax increase failed on a bipartisan procedural vote with the following senators blocking the advancement: Sen. Andersen, Sen. Ballard, Sen. John Cavanaugh, Sen. Conrad, Sen. Guereca, Sen. Juarez, Sen. McKinney, Sen. Rountree, Sen. Storm, and Sen. Strommen. The Platte Institute is sympathetic in the...

The post Nebraska Cigarette Tax Increase Would Raise Prices, Reduce Revenue Reliability, and Increase Smuggling appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] First They Came for the Data Centers, But Will It Stop There?
item.description:
Building Virginia into the leader of the data center industry for the United States and the world has been a bipartisan goal of several recent governors and General Assemblies. The golden egg laying goose they nurtured is now in danger of being butchered. What is happening to this one industry during the 2026 General Assembly […]

The post First They Came for the Data Centers, But Will It Stop There? appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Empowerms] Empower MS Releases 2025 Impact Report
item.description:
As we look back on 2025, we are reminded how much can change when Mississippians come together around a simple conviction: every person, no matter their ZIP code, income, or background, deserves the opportunity to rise. Last year brought meaningful progress across education, work, and justice. Mississippi continued to earn national attention for its academic…

The post Empower MS Releases 2025 Impact Report first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Mass. Officials Talk a Big Game About Democracy. Do They Practice What They Preach?
item.description:
Elected officials in Massachusetts sure have a lot to say about “democracy” these days.   For those paying attention to goings on around here in recent years, hearing politicians trumpeting their state as a citadel defending democracy and the rule of law, you might be...

The post Mass. Officials Talk a Big Game About Democracy. Do They Practice What They Preach?  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] LB 663: Nebraska Agricultural Zoning Reform Improves County Permitting Process
item.description:
On February 25, 2026, Governor Jim Pillen signed Legislative Bill 663 into law while at the Nebraska Dairy Convention and Trade Show in West Point, Nebraska. Introduced by Senator Tanya Storer, and championed by The Platte Institute, LB 663 improves county zoning processes for agricultural producers by establishing a more uniform, transparent, and timely system...

The post LB 663: Nebraska Agricultural Zoning Reform Improves County Permitting Process appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Justice Pierce Butler: Minnesota’s Forgotten Defender of Constitutional Conservatism
item.description:
Justice Oliver Wendell Holmes described his colleague Justice Pierce Butler as a “monolith, there are no seems that the frost can get through. He is of one piece.” Justice Louis Brandeis regarded him as “one of the most powerful on [the] bench.” William D. Mitchell, who served as Butler’s law partner and later Attorney General in President Herbert Hoover’s administration, described him as a “dominating figure.” Justice Pierce Butler was often described by his contemporaries by both his physical size and his intellect. Justice Holmes reference to Butler as monolithic is appropriate for the Justice’s size, but more importantly for his unwavering commitment to principle or constitutional conservatism.

The post Justice Pierce Butler: Minnesota’s Forgotten Defender of Constitutional Conservatism appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Dr. Clay Routledge: Meaning, Purpose, and Flourishing
item.description:
Dr. Clay Routledge: Meaning, Purpose, and Flourishing In this episode of The Empower Podcast, host Grant Callen sits down with Dr. Clay Routledge, Executive Vice President of the Archbridge Institute and Director of the Human Flourishing Lab, to explore what really makes life meaningful and why that matters for public policy in Mississippi and beyond.…

The post Dr. Clay Routledge: Meaning, Purpose, and Flourishing first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] NEW POLL: Louisiana Voters Sound the Alarm on Rising Costs, Demand School Choice Funding and Legal Reform as Legislative Session Begins; Carnival Season Debate Adds Local Flavor
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Media Contact: sydney@pelicaninstitute.org March 3, 2026 BATON ROUGE, LA—As Louisiana lawmakers convene for the 2026 legislative session, the Pelican Institute for Public Policy today released its Q1 2026 Pelican Pulse poll, revealing deep voter concern over rising utility costs, broad support for school choice and government restraint, and clear demand for legal and...

The post NEW POLL: Louisiana Voters Sound the Alarm on Rising Costs, Demand School Choice Funding and Legal Reform as Legislative Session Begins; Carnival Season Debate Adds Local Flavor appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Race and Crime: Stats Matter (He) “struggled with mental health issues” and appeared to have “suffered a crisis.” — the mayor of Charlotte describing the man who stabbed to death 23-year-old Iryna Zarutska riding the train to her work. A FRIEND, a young man with a wife and two small children, was shot a few days Read the full article...

item.title: [Jbartlett] A Conval response: The state funds academics, districts fund supports
item.description:
“Whatever the State identifies as comprising constitutional adequacy it must pay for.” — N.H. Supreme Court, Londonderry School District SAU #12 v. State of New Hampshire, 2006   Since the state lost its first Claremont education funding case in 1993, legislators and governors (including Democratic Gov. John Lynch in 2011-2012) tried to undo the decision […]

The post A Conval response: The state funds academics, districts fund supports appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Mackinac] ‘SAFE’ bills are not safe
item.description: Michigan tech package would violate U.S. Constitution and do little to protect children

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Warns School Districts and Alerts Parents After Supreme Court Affirms Parents’ Rights in Schools
item.description:
WILL has provided new legal guidance to school districts and parents following the Supreme Court’s recent order in Mirabelli v. Bonta, which addressed parental rights in the context of school policies concerning student gender identity.

The post WILL Warns School Districts and Alerts Parents After Supreme Court Affirms Parents’ Rights in Schools appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Beacontn] Why Tennessee Doesn’t Need a State Property Tax
item.description: We’ve made it just fine for more than 75 years without a state property tax, and we’ll be just fine in the future without one.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed
item.description:
The following appeared in the Albuquerque Journal on March 2nd, 2026 and in other newspapers across New Mexico. The passage of medical malpractice reform is the most important public policy success in New Mexico in more than a decade. It took support from New Mexicans of every political stripe and a bipartisan coalition in the Legislature to […]

The post Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] In Today’s Richmond Times-Dispatch: Students Can’t Afford the Learning Loss that Will Come from Paid Leave Bill
item.description:
The Richmond Times-Dispatch ran my guest column this morning that warns of the significant learning loss that will likely occur from the Paid Family and Medical Leave bill (SB2) that is quickly winding its way to the Governror’s desk for her promised signature. As I have written previously, the Virginia General Assembly is about to pass the most […]

The post In Today’s Richmond Times-Dispatch: Students Can’t Afford the Learning Loss that Will Come from Paid Leave Bill appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] An income tax for New Hampshire? Delaware & Connecticut offer a warning
item.description:
Socialist gadfly Andru Volinsky held a press conference on Tuesday to propose a dual broad-based tax for New Hampshire: a 3% state income tax paired with a “true 3% statewide property tax.” Pitched as a tax cut, his plan would raise $1 billion in state tax revenue, he said.  How would a new income tax […]

The post An income tax for New Hampshire? Delaware & Connecticut offer a warning appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Needs to Cut the Red Tape for Rural Hospitals
item.description:
Alabama’s rural hospitals are struggling, and our state’s bureaucratic red tape isn’t helping. The Alabama Hospital Association reports 27 hospitals in danger of closing and 19 in immediate risk.

The post Alabama Needs to Cut the Red Tape for Rural Hospitals appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Happy parents, unequal opportunities
item.description: Parents in Idaho and Montana are among the most satisfied in the country with their children’s schools. In Montana, 51% of parents say they are very satisfied with their child’s school, well above the national average of 47%. Idaho parents report similar confidence, with 48% saying they are very satisfied.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama’s most influential political groups converge as ‘Freedom First Alabama’ to wage war on big government policies
item.description:
One of the most powerful political coalitions ever assembled in state history was launched today, bringing the state’s most influential industry and advocacy groups together with a singular purpose: to go on offense against policies that will raise costs for families and employers in Alabama.

The post Alabama’s most influential political groups converge as ‘Freedom First Alabama’ to wage war on big government policies appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Federal Court Orders Government Transparency over $37.7 Million Grand Canyon University Fine
item.description:
In 2023, the U.S. Department of Education issued the largest fine in the agency’s history—a staggering $37.7 million—against Phoenix-based Grand..

The post Federal Court Orders Government Transparency over $37.7 Million Grand Canyon University Fine appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Choice and Charter Schools Outperform Public Schools, New Report Shows
item.description:
Students attending Wisconsin’s private choice and charter schools outperform their peers in traditional public schools in both English Language Arts (ELA) and math, even after accounting for demographic differences, according to a new Apples to Apples report released by WILL.

The post Choice and Charter Schools Outperform Public Schools, New Report Shows appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Why Kansas Wins When SCOTUS Stops Tariff Overreach
item.description: Kansas cannot treat trade as a talking point. Trade is a pillar of the state’s economy, from Wichita manufacturing to rural agriculture. The state’s Kansas International Trade Summary shows how closely Kansas jobs and incomes are tied to global markets, and the Kansas state profile from the U.S. Trade Representative highlights the scale of exports […]

item.description:

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] You don’t need your union’s permission to opt out
item.description:
Freedom Foundation’s Elite Canvassing Team witnessed a moment that revealed exactly why so many public employees quietly say they feel pressured inside their own workplaces.

The post You don’t need your union’s permission to opt out appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Idaho House Passes Freedom Foundation-Backed Bill Ending Taxpayer Support for Teachers Unions
item.description:
The Idaho House of Representatives has passed legislation backed by the Freedom Foundation that would end taxpayer support for teachers unions.

The post Idaho House Passes Freedom Foundation-Backed Bill Ending Taxpayer Support for Teachers Unions   appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Building the Abundant Water Coalition
item.description:
If enough people in California agreed on a state water strategy, the political obstacles would be overcome. If every major water agency, every farming association, and a critical mass of environmental groups were all committed to a specific set of policies and projects, then elected politicians would be bound to adhere to those priorities. Regulatory...

The post Building the Abundant Water Coalition first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Braid Against the Machine: Louisiana’s 600-Hour Money Grab
item.description:
The number of states eliminating or reforming their requirements to braid natural hair has increased over the last few years. Today, 37 states no longer require a permit to braid hair, freeing entrepreneurs to earn a living using a skill that is often passed down through generations of women. Unfortunately, Louisiana loves to buck a...

The post Braid Against the Machine: Louisiana’s 600-Hour Money Grab appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: A Key Legislative Deadline — and a Bill to Help Mississippi’s Independent Workers
item.description:
Yesterday marked one of the most important procedural deadlines of the legislative session. It was the deadline for committees to report general bills and constitutional amendments originating in the other chamber. While it may sound technical, this deadline has a very real impact on which policies still have a chance of becoming law this year. Here’s […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: A Key Legislative Deadline — and a Bill to Help Mississippi’s Independent Workers appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Asymmetric Advantages of Environmentalist Zealotry
item.description:
With the world anxiously watching the conflict in Iran, it was no surprise that the first segment in the March 1 edition of CBS’s 60 Minutes featured an interview with Reza Pahlavi, the exiled son of Iran’s last Shah. The second segment, however, returned to a staple theme of the CBS news team. It presented...

The post The Asymmetric Advantages of Environmentalist Zealotry first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.description:
New York has some of the highest household energy prices in the nation, according to Empire Center and U.S. Energy....

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Stephanie Smith: The $100 million myth vs. Alabama’s $12 billion reality
item.description:
Last week, during a "State of the Schools" event in North Alabama, leaders from Huntsville City, Madison City, and Madison County schools sounded a familiar but mathematically creative alarm.

item.title: [Mackinac] Cigarette smuggling increase on the docket in Washington State
item.description: Excise will lead to double-digit spike in avoidance and evasion

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Income tax cut repeal could stunt business growth and job creation
item.description:
The following testimony was submitted by the Grassroot Institute of Hawaii for consideration by the House Committee on Ways and Means on March 5, 2026._____________ March 5, 2026, 10:05 a.m.Hawaii State CapitolConference Room 211 and Videoconference To: House Committee on Ways and Means       Sen. Donovan M. Dela Cruz, Chair       Sen. Sharon Y. Moriwaki, Vice Chair […]

The post Income tax cut repeal could stunt business growth and job creation appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Economic reports show Washington state failing the test
item.description: The reality today is that policymakers are making it increasingly harder to thrive and innovate within the ever-increasing tax and regulatory burden. The numerous economic reports are a loud warning that, unless a course correction is made, more entrepreneurs will be looking elsewhere to do business.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Low Turnout, High Approval: 11 of 12 School Tax Measures Pass in March Special Election
item.description:
Turnout in the March special election remained extremely low across the participating districts. Across the 12 contests, just 6,336 votes were cast out of 136,901 registered voters — a turnout of only 4.6 percent. Individual district turnout varied significantly. The highest turnout occurred in Sibley-Ocheyedan CSD, where 34.8 percent of registered voters participated in the PPEL renewal election. The lowest turnout occurred in Maquoketa CSD, where just 2.1 percent of registered voters cast ballots. Even in districts where turnout was higher than expected — such as South Winneshiek and Decorah CSDs — participation remained well below the levels seen during recent bond elections, where turnout reached 48.6 percent and 73 percent respectively. While nearly all measures passed, the extremely low turnout means that long-term tax decisions affecting multiple school districts were determined by only a small fraction of eligible voters.

The post Low Turnout, High Approval: 11 of 12 School Tax Measures Pass in March Special Election appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Beyond the Yard Signs: Looking for Principled Policy in Louisiana Candidates
item.description:
Numerous political races are currently underway in Louisiana, which can be both exciting and overwhelming as voters attempt to stay informed. Beyond the labels, slogans, and talking points lie real policy issues in need of principled solutions. As Louisiana heads into the 2026 Public Service Commission race, energy and infrastructure policy will be front and...

item.title: [Johnlocke] Newsom turns against Israel for political purpose
item.description:
Ira Stoll writes for the Washington Free Beacon about the California governor’s latest transformation. The governor of California, Gavin Newsom, a likely 2028 Democratic presidential candidate, is abandoning his support for Israel now that he thinks it has become politically inconvenient. That’s the takeaway from Newsom’s March 3, 2026, book-promotion appearance with two former aides...

Phil Berger is currently trailing Sam Page, with the former having 13,075 votes and the latter...

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Failed Los Angeles fire recovery proves need for self-governance
item.description:
But why? In Los Angeles, and the rest of the state’s major cities, the public sector has become the city’s dominant political force, drowning out the votes and priorities of everyday citizens. With this imbalance making local politics an often-forgone conclusion, it’s time to consider a possible reform that could restore the competitive opportunity for real, effective change. […]

The post Failed Los Angeles fire recovery proves need for self-governance appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] When the Tax Code Becomes a Policy Weapon
item.description:
Some states are funding rising spending with wealth taxes and targeted levies instead of restraining government growth. This marks a shift away from broad, neutral tax policy. A growing divide is emerging between states expanding government and those prioritizing tax competitiveness. Policy choices today will shape migration, investment, and job growth tomorrow. Targeted taxes, including those on firearms, highlight the risk of using the tax code to steer behavior or place financial pressure on constitutional rights. The implications extend beyond revenue to fundamental freedoms.

The post When the Tax Code Becomes a Policy Weapon appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] ‘All of your data has been stolen’
item.description: Who's stealing your tax dollars? Haywood Talcove has the answer, and it's worse than you think

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Delineating ‘Growth OUR MAYOR likes to tout her city as being one of the fastest “growing’ in the state. The scare quotes are to alert you that growth needs some definition. The mayor is talking demographically, that is, her city is increasing in the raw number of people. That is the wrong metric, it will be argued Read the full article...

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Examining Local Government Spending Trends From 2014 to 2023
item.description:
City and county spending has outpaced growth in population and inflation, leading to an increase in the tax burden on Texan taxpayers. Key points: Local government spending continues to outstrip increases in both population and inflation. From 2014 to 2023, local government spending rose by 61.2%, whereas population and inflation increased by only 42.7%. Elevated...

item.description:

Expanding access to patient-owned health savings accounts and routing federal subsidies through them, rather than through insurance companies, would unleash competition by empowering consumers to spend their healthcare dollars as they see fit. So would stronger price transparency requirements. But one proposal the […]

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] One job, two standards: Idaho’s electrician licensing puzzle
item.description: A new policy analysis released today by the Mountain States Policy Center raises questions about Idaho’s licensing requirements for journeyman electricians, finding that the state requires additional classroom hours that are not required in several other states with which Idaho maintains license reciprocity.

Conversely, does it decrease government spending or debt? House Bill 840 would amend Section 63-2552A, Idaho Code, […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Senate Bill 1365 — Campaign finance, religious orgs (0)
item.description: Bill Description: Senate Bill 1365 would exempt a religious organization that supports or opposes ballot measures from donor disclosure requirements under Idaho’s campaign finance law, so long as it spends less than 10% of its total receipts on such efforts. Rating: 0 Does it violate the spirit or the letter of either the United States Constitution […]

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Assessing Tax Trends in Denton County
item.description:
Does history suggest that property taxes are too high in Denton County? And, if so, have local decision-makers actively helped or hurt the situation?   To help answer these questions, let’s review the county’s 2024 Annual Comprehensive Financial Report (ACFR) and gather four types of data—i.e., tax levies, local population, total tax rates, and taxable values—over a 10-year time horizon....

The post Assessing Tax Trends in Denton County first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.
